zh.London, City of London, Greater London,: 英國英格蘭倫敦布倫登
zh.Barry, Vale of Glamorgan, Glamorgan, Wal: 英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根巴里
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Barry於1400年出生在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根巴里時，他的父親Sir Thomas de la Barre33歲，母親Alice Talbot12歲。他於大約1420年與Margaret Wight結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1421年在英國英格蘭倫敦布倫登享年21歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Barry于1400年出生在英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根巴里时，他的父亲Sir Thomas de la Barre33岁，母亲Alice Talbot12岁。他于大约1420年与Margaret Wight结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1421年在英国英格兰倫敦布倫登享年21岁。

nameMeanings.name: Barry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Donal, Aidan, Ciaran, Eoin, John Patrick, Kaitlin, Kieran, Murphy, Aileen, Brigid, Caitlin.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Beargha ‘descendant of Beargh’, a byname meaning ‘plunderer’.

Irish and Manx: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Báire ‘descendant of Báire’, a short form of either of two Gaelic personal names, Bairrfhionn or Fionnbharr, both meaning ‘fair-topped’. This may also be the source of the Manx name Barry, whose family's main seat was Ballavarry in Kirk Andreas.

variants.name: Barr
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow) and northern Irish (Ulster): habitational name from any of various places in southwestern Scotland, in particular Ayrshire and Renfrewshire, named with Gaelic barr ‘height, hill’ or a Brittonic cognate of this.

English and Welsh: habitational name from Great Barr in Staffordshire. This is from Brittonic barro- ‘top, summit’, referring to Barr Beacon; there is evidence that this was known as la Bare in the 13th century.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Barre-en-Ouche in Eure, France, or perhaps from Barre-de-Semilly in Manche, France.

variants.name: Barrie
meanings: Scottish (Angus): habitational name from Barry in Angus or from another place so named, generally named with Gaelic barr ‘height, hill’, or from a British cognate of this word. This name is also the Scottish form of Barry .
French (Barrié): variant of Barrier .
Altered form of French   Baril  . Compare   Barry   7.

variants.name: Berry
meanings: Irish and Manx: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Béara ‘descendant of Béara’, a personal name of unexplained etymology; or, in some cases, perhaps an Anglicized form of Irish and Manx Ó Beargha. Compare Barry 1.
Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Barrie  .
English: habitational name from any of several places called in Devon named with Old English byrig, dative case of burh ‘fortified manor house, stronghold’, such as Berry Pomeroy and Berrynarbor.

variants.name: Dubarry
meanings: French: variant of the topographic or habitational name Barry , with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’.

variants.name: Baril
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Normand, Alain, Armand, Ghislaine, Jacques, Jean-Marc, Jean-Pierre, Lucien, Monique, Phillippe.
French: from Old French baril ‘barrel’, applied as a metonymic occupational name for a cooper, or a nickname for a rotund man. Compare Barry 7 and Berry 7.

History: Jean Baril from the Saintonge region of France was in QC by 1673, when he married Marie Guillet, the first of his three wives. He died in 1724 in Batiscan, QC. His descendants also bear altered forms of the surname, such as Barrie, Bar(r)y, and Berry.

variants.name: Bary
meanings: Walloon and northern French: variant of   Barry   8.
French (southern): rare variant of   Barry   6.
Altered form of French   Baril  . Compare   Barry   7.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Barry was born in 1400, in Barry, Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Thomas de la Barre, was 33 and his mother, Alice Talbot, was 12. He married Margaret Wight about 1420. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1421, in Blendon, London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 21.

fr.Barry, Vale of Glamorgan, Glamorgan, Wal: Barry, Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Barry est né en 1400, à Barry, Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Thomas de la Barre, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Alice Talbot, avait 12 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Wight environ 1420. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1421, à Blendon, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 21 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Barry 1400 in Barry, Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Thomas de la Barre 33 und seine Mutter Alice Talbot 12. Er hat um 1420 Margaret Wight geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1421 im Alter von 21 Jahren in Blendon, London, England, United Kingdom.

Lui sposato Margaret Wight circa 1420. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1421, nel Blendon, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 21 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Barry(이)가 1400년 영국 웨일스 글램르건 배리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas de la Barre(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Talbot(은)는 12세였다. 그는 대략 1420년 Margaret Wight(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1421년 영국 잉글랜드 런던 블렌던에서 21세의 나이로 사망했다.
